summary: AFI's reference spine (ingest -> scoring DAG -> evidence vault -> mint -> on-chain commitment) is implemented by a concrete chain of repos: afi-gateway (HTTP ingest, vault.upsert), afi-reactor (Froggy DAG via runPipeline/runPipelineDag) feeding afi-core/afi-plugins/afi-tiny-brains nodes, afi-infra (canonical VaultedSignalRecord + TSSD clients), afi-mint (off-chain MintExecutor.mintForSignal coordination), and afi-token (on-chain AFIMintCoordinator.mintForSignal / AFIToken.mintEmissions). This matches the recon corpus classification of all 31 repos (afi-config + afi-infra NORMATIVE; gateway/reactor/core/plugins/tiny-brains/mint/token/governance REFERENCE_IMPL; econ/labs RESEARCH; agents/construct/sdk-* STALE; xerc20/research-site OUT_OF_SCOPE; the rest SUPPORTING/DOCS). Two reference-implementation choices are widely presented as if they were protocol law: MongoDB (vault.schema.json advertises four engines but only a MongoDB client exists, and afi-infra hard-fails in production without a Mongo URI) and afi-reactor (docs repeatedly call it 'the ONLY orchestrator' and 'the DAG is law'). Of the four schema-listed vault engines (mongodb/postgresql/timescaledb/influxdb), only mongodb is actually implemented; postgresql/timescaledb/influxdb are schema-listed-only with zero client code.

answers.question: B1: Which repos implement the reference spine (ingest -> scoring DAG -> evidence vault -> mint -> on-chain commitment)?
answers.answer: The reference spine maps to a concrete chain of repos, each owning one segment. INGEST boundary = afi-gateway: POST /api/v1/signals (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:123) builds a VaultedSignalRecord via normalizeSignalPayload (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:20) and persists it with vault.upsert (afi-gateway/src/http/app.ts:134); the vault is a TenantScoped->Mongo client wired in afi-gateway/src/services/vaultFactory.ts:24-33. SCORING DAG = afi-reactor: the deterministic Froggy pipeline executor runPipeline / runPipelineDag (afi-reactor/src/services/pipelineRunner.ts:161; afi-reactor/src/services/pipelineRunner.ts:339), which orchestrates plugin/analyst nodes drawn from afi-core (UWR scorer afi-core/validators/UniversalWeightingRule.ts), afi-plugins (stub node registry), and afi-tiny-brains (HTTP ML enrichment, called by afi-reactor per afi-tiny-brains/README.md:11). EVIDENCE VAULT = afi-infra: canonical VaultedSignalRecord (afi-infra/src/tssd/types.ts:331) and the ITSSDVaultClient storage contract (afi-infra/src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts:36-68). MINT coordination (off-chain) = afi-mint: MintExecutor.mintForSignal (afi-mint/src/orchestrator/MintExecutor.ts:33) which calls the on-chain coordinator (afi-mint/src/orchestrator/MintExecutor.ts:108). ON-CHAIN COMMITMENT = afi-token: AFIMintCoordinator.mintForSignal (afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:68) -> AFIToken.mintEmissions (afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:92). Normative surface underpinning the spine = afi-config (schemas: USS/CPJ/vault/pipeline) and afi-infra (TSSD types/spec). This is exactly the spine recorded in the draft map (afi-docs/specs/audit/drafts/AFI_REFERENCE_IMPL_MAP.draft.md:13-24).

answers.question: B2: How does the corpus classify all 31 repos, and is the theme-B reference-spine map consistent with it?
answers.answer: The recon corpus (31 records, confirmed by init.sh 'corpus records: 31') classifies the repos as: NORMATIVE = afi-config, afi-infra (the protocol-law schema/type owners); REFERENCE_IMPL = afi-gateway, afi-reactor, afi-core, afi-plugins, afi-tiny-brains, afi-mint, afi-token, afi-governance (the working spine + governance plane); RESEARCH = afi-econ, afi-labs; STALE = afi-construct, afi-agents (archived), afi-sdk-python, afi-sdk-ts (empty scaffolds); OUT_OF_SCOPE = afi-xerc20 (vendored defi-wonderland fork), afi-research-site (marketing site); DOCS = afi-docs, afi-protocol; SUPPORTING = .github, afi-artifacts, afi-assets, afi-benchkit, afi-cli-framework, afi-cli-shared, afi-factory, afi-math, afi-ops, afi-skills, afi-starters. The B1 spine map is fully consistent with this: every spine segment is owned by a REFERENCE_IMPL repo except the two NORMATIVE anchors (afi-config schemas, afi-infra TSSD types), and no STALE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/RESEARCH repo sits on the spine. afi-econ's epoch-pool gauge splits are correctly held off the spine (RESEARCH), matching Theme G's finding that on-chain mint is single-beneficiary.

answers.question: B3: Where does code/docs imply MongoDB is mandatory (rather than a reference/default vault engine)?
answers.answer: Mongo is the reference Evidence-plane engine but several places treat it as mandatory. In afi-infra the production factory hard-fails without a Mongo URI: 'AFI_TSSD_MONGODB_URI is required in production. Falling back to in-memory is not allowed.' (afi-infra/src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts:197-201), so any conforming operator on a different engine cannot run the reference infra in prod. The only persistent ITSSDVaultClient is Mongo-backed (afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:72 implements ITSSDVaultClient; dynamic import('mongodb') + new MongoClient at afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:220-221). The gateway wires Mongo as the only vault backend (afi-gateway/src/services/vaultFactory.ts:24-33; afi-gateway/src/lib/db/mongo.ts:43-52 throws if MONGODB_URI unset). The reactor's scored-signal persistence is Mongo-only and requires AFI_MONGO_URI (afi-reactor/src/services/tssdVaultService.ts:6,59). Ops/starters templates bake Mongo in as required infra (afi-ops/.afi-codex.json external mongodb 'required: true'; afi-starters/self-hosted-pipeline/docker-compose.yml ships a mongo:7 service). The TSSD spec also presents Mongo time-series as THE backing store (afi-infra/docs/TSSD_VAULT_SPEC.md:98). Per the North Star, Mongo is a reference path, not protocol law, so these are P2 'reference-as-law' tensions, not protocol violations.

answers.question: B4: Where does code/docs imply afi-reactor (and org infra) is the mandatory orchestrator (rather than the reference orchestrator)?
answers.answer: Multiple authoritative docs present afi-reactor as THE orchestrator, not A reference orchestrator. afi-reactor/README.md:137 'afi-reactor is the ONLY orchestrator in AFI Protocol. All canonical pipelines, DAGs, and routing logic live here.'; afi-reactor/AGENTS.md:3 '...This is the ONLY orchestrator in the AFI ecosystem-agents are nodes, not orchestrators.'; the Orchestrator Doctrine asserts 'The DAG is law. Every signal path ... must be expressible as a Reactor DAG; ad-hoc flows are anti-patterns' (afi-reactor/docs/AFI_ORCHESTRATOR_DOCTRINE.md, mirrored in afi-docs/specs/audit/drafts/AFI_REFERENCE_IMPL_MAP.draft.md:447). The reactor-as-canonical-orchestrator framing is echoed by downstream repos as binding: afi-core/docs/AFI_CORE_RUNTIME_OVERVIEW.md:39 'AFI-Reactor (already hardened) is the canonical orchestrator for AFI Protocol.'; afi-config governance charter afi-config/codex/governance/droids/AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md:117 names afi-reactor 'the orchestrator'; afi-protocol/architecture_overview.md:23 lists Reactor as 'DAG orchestration (source of truth)'. Per the North Star (afi-docs/specs/AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md:3.4) the protocol law is conforming DAG outputs + pinned versions, and any conforming orchestrator is allowed, so 'ONLY orchestrator' is reference-as-law (P2), not a normative rule.

answers.question: B5: Which vault engines are actually implemented vs only listed in vault.schema.json?
answers.answer: vault.schema.json advertises a four-engine, pluggable Evidence plane: the 'engine' enum is ['mongodb','postgresql','timescaledb','influxdb'] (afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:14-23). In code, only mongodb is implemented. Exactly three classes implement ITSSDVaultClient in afi-infra: MongoTSSDVaultClient (afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:72, a real persistent Mongo time-series client, import('mongodb')+new MongoClient at :220-221), InMemoryTSSDVaultClient (afi-infra/src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts:90, a non-persistent dev/test stub with 'No persistence, encryption, or access control'), and TenantScopedTSSDVaultClient (afi-infra/src/tssd/TenantScopedTSSDVaultClient.ts:19, a wrapper that defaults its inner client to MongoTSSDVaultClient at :25 - it is not its own engine). A workspace grep for postgres/timescale/influx/pg client code in afi-infra/src returns zero engine clients (only the Mongo and InMemory clients exist), and the env-driven factory only ever returns InMemory or Mongo (afi-infra/src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts:207,218). So postgresql/timescaledb/influxdb are SCHEMA-LISTED ONLY (no implementation); mongodb is the sole implemented persistent engine, with InMemory as a non-persistent test fallback.

answers.question: B6: Is the on-chain commitment segment of the spine actually afi-mint or afi-token, and are there stale repos that should not be read as spine implementations?
answers.answer: The on-chain commitment layer is implemented ONLY by afi-token/src (3 Solidity contracts: AFIToken, AFIMintCoordinator, AFISignalReceipt); afi-mint owns the OFF-CHAIN coordination FSM and merely calls the coordinator via an interface (afi-mint/src/orchestrator/MintExecutor.ts:108 this.contract.mintForSignal(request)). afi-mint/contracts are empty stubs (per Theme C), and afi-xerc20 is a vendored defi-wonderland bridge fork (OUT_OF_SCOPE), so neither should be read as the AFI commitment layer. Several repos carry STALE names/refs that must not be mistaken for live spine code: afi-agents is GitHub-archived (afi-docs/specs/audit/drafts/AFI_REFERENCE_IMPL_MAP.draft.md:52), afi-construct is archived, and afi-sdk-python/afi-sdk-ts are empty scaffolds (README-only, no src) still advertised as 'API clients for afi-reactor' (afi-sdk-python/README.md:11). Legacy names afi-engine / afi-token-finalized appear as downstream labels in afi-math metadata but are not live repos. These belong to Theme J's docs-drift expansion.

findings.title: Reference Evidence-plane vault is MongoDB-only while vault.schema.json advertises four pluggable engines
findings.evidence: afi-config/schemas/vault.schema.json:14-23 engine enum ['mongodb','postgresql','timescaledb','influxdb']; only afi-infra/src/tssd/MongoTSSDVaultClient.ts:72 implements ITSSDVaultClient as a persistent engine (import('mongodb') at :220-221); no postgres/timescale/influx client exists in afi-infra/src
findings.recommendation: Document postgresql/timescaledb/influxdb as schema-listed-but-unimplemented in AFI_REFERENCE_IMPL_MAP.md and keep ITSSDVaultClient as the engine-neutral seam; either provide at least one non-Mongo reference binding or annotate the schema enum that only mongodb is implemented today.

findings.title: afi-infra production code hard-requires a Mongo URI, making the reference vault engine effectively mandatory in prod
findings.evidence: afi-infra/src/tssd/TSSDVaultClient.ts:197-201 'AFI_TSSD_MONGODB_URI is required in production. Falling back to in-memory is not allowed.'; afi-gateway/src/lib/db/mongo.ts:43-52 throws if MONGODB_URI unset; afi-ops/.afi-codex.json external mongodb 'required: true'
findings.recommendation: Reframe the production guard as 'a persistent ITSSDVaultClient is required in production' (engine-agnostic) so the reference infra does not encode Mongo as protocol-mandatory; carry into the contradiction register under the Mongo-only tension.

findings.title: Docs present afi-reactor as 'the ONLY orchestrator' and 'the DAG is law', presenting the reference orchestrator as protocol law
findings.evidence: afi-reactor/README.md:137 'afi-reactor is the ONLY orchestrator in AFI Protocol.'; afi-reactor/AGENTS.md:3 'This is the ONLY orchestrator in the AFI ecosystem'; afi-core/docs/AFI_CORE_RUNTIME_OVERVIEW.md:39 'AFI-Reactor ... is the canonical orchestrator for AFI Protocol.'
findings.recommendation: Reframe reactor as the reference orchestrator: protocol law is conforming DAG outputs + pinned transform versions (per AFI_PORTABLE_PROTOCOL_SURFACE.v0.1.md 3.4); add a banner that any conforming orchestrator (Mage blocks, custom DAG) is permitted. Cross-link Theme E (reactor-as-only-orchestrator quotes).

findings.title: Stale/empty repos still advertised as spine participants (afi-agents archived; afi-sdk-python/afi-sdk-ts empty scaffolds claiming reactor clients)
findings.evidence: afi-sdk-python/README.md:11 '- Pythonic API clients for afi-reactor' (repo has only README + pyproject, no src); afi-docs/specs/audit/drafts/AFI_REFERENCE_IMPL_MAP.draft.md:52 afi-agents PRIVATE STALE (GitHub-archived)
findings.recommendation: Tag afi-agents/afi-construct as archived and afi-sdk-* as unimplemented scaffolds in the reference-impl map so they are not mistaken for live spine segments; defer detailed drift cataloguing to Theme J.

findings.title: On-chain commitment is afi-token/src only; afi-mint coordinates off-chain and afi-xerc20 is a vendored fork (do not read either as the commitment layer)
findings.evidence: afi-mint/src/orchestrator/MintExecutor.ts:108 calls this.contract.mintForSignal (interface, off-chain); afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:68 mintForSignal onlyRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE) is the on-chain implementation
findings.recommendation: In the reference-impl map, label afi-mint as the off-chain mint-coordination segment and afi-token/src as the sole on-chain commitment segment; classify afi-xerc20 OUT_OF_SCOPE (vendored bridge). Cross-link Theme C/G.

contradictions.doc_says: vault.schema.json defines the Evidence plane as engine-pluggable across mongodb/postgresql/timescaledb/influxdb.
contradictions.code_does: Only a MongoDB time-series client implements ITSSDVaultClient; postgresql/timescaledb/influxdb have no client code, and afi-infra throws in production without a Mongo URI.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-reactor docs and the Orchestrator Doctrine assert afi-reactor is THE only orchestrator and 'the DAG is law' for every signal path.
contradictions.code_does: afi-reactor is one concrete TypeScript DAG executor (runPipeline/runPipelineDag); the portable-protocol charter says any conforming orchestrator producing conforming, version-pinned outputs is valid.

contradictions.doc_says: TSSD_VAULT_SPEC and ops/starters present MongoDB as the (required) backing store for the reference stack.
contradictions.code_does: MongoDB is described in the North Star as a reference/default vault path, not protocol law, yet prod infra and templates make it mandatory.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-sdk-python/afi-sdk-ts advertise official API clients for afi-reactor, and older docs reference afi-agents/afi-engine as live components.
contradictions.code_does: afi-sdk-python/afi-sdk-ts are empty README-only scaffolds with no source; afi-agents is GitHub-archived and afi-engine is a legacy name with no repo.
